rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The W.W. Henry Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thusday, October 12, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant The W.W. Henry Company, L.P.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the issue of whether the Henry roofing product at issue contained asbestos, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's Separate Statement did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his contention of liability. Additionally, the deposition testimony excerpts fail to demonstrate that plaintiff does not know the number designation for the Henry's product at issue. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. The declaration of Lori Angelo is insufficient to demonstrate that the Henry's product at issue was more likely than not a non-asbestos-containing product. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Dr. Richard Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether exposure to defendant's product was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's risk of developing cancer. An expert declaration in opposition to a summary judgment motion must be liberally construed. (Garrett v. Howmedica Osteonics Corp. (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 173.) Plaintiff's objections numbers 1, 2, 3 and 6 are sustained and numbers 4, 5 and 7 are overruled. Defendant's objections to Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thusday, October 12, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Triple A Machine Shop, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the Negligence II cause of action and off calendar as to the Negligence I and Products Liability causes of action. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The excerpts of witness Frank Taforo's deposition testimony submitted by defendant are not sufficient to demonstrate that Mr. Taforo does not have knowledge supporting plaintiff's claims. This precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant also failed to sustain its initial burden because it did not demonstrate that plaintiff's Negligence II cause of action must fail because plaintiff did not work at a Triple A premise. Plaintiff has dismissed his first (Negligence I) and second (Products Liability) causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
